description:
[1x] Strawberry Tree (Arbutus unedo) @ Aquatic Park

They are best picked when the skins of the berries are very red and they are a bit soft to the touch.

The berries are ready for harvest when very mature, which is to say very red and slightly giving to the touch.

description:
[1x] Black Walnut (Juglans nigra) @ Aquatic Park

Harvest walnuts when they fall to the ground. 

Hulls soften naturally over time, allowing easy access to the nut. If the hull feels firm and is difficult to remove, set the nut aside for a few days.

description:
[1x] White Mulberry (Morus alba) @ 1276 65th St

Mulberries are ripe when they come off the tree easily, when they are plump and at full dark purple/red, or a full white (not bright red or green). The easiest way to harvest is to shake the branches to encourage ripe Mulberries to fall to the ground.

The white mulberry fruit's high sugar content, around 10 percent, makes it a favorite for making pies, jellies, jams and syrups, and these berries are sweet and juicy when eaten raw at peak ripeness. These fruits ripen quickly, requiring them to be eaten right away, frozen, or used in recipes.
author: City of Berkeley

description:
[1x] Date Palm (Phoenix dactylifera) @ 1449 66th St

Its fruit is edible fresh but is very bitter if eaten before it is ripe. You can dry the fruits in the sun and preserve them for a long time. Young date leaves can be cooked and eaten as a vegetable.
author: City of Berkeley
